title: APAC Cross-Border Advisory Disclosure
regulatory_ref: MAS FAA Section 27, SFC Code of Conduct
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS - APAC CROSS-BORDER ADVISORY DISCLOSURE

This disclosure document outlines the regulatory framework and compliance requirements for cross-border wealth management services provided by Meridian Wealth Partners across the Asia-Pacific region. All associates and above must familiarize themselves with these jurisdictional requirements prior to engaging with clients across APAC markets.

REGULATORY FRAMEWORK

Our APAC operations are governed by multiple regulatory authorities, primarily the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) under the Financial Advisers Act Section 27, and the Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) Code of Conduct in Hong Kong. These regulations establish strict requirements for cross-border advisory services, including client suitability assessments, product disclosure obligations, and ongoing monitoring responsibilities.

Under MAS FAA Section 27, all investment recommendations must be accompanied by comprehensive risk disclosures, particularly for clients with investable assets below SGD 2 million. The regulation mandates that associates conduct enhanced due diligence for cross-border transactions exceeding USD 500,000 or involving complex structured products with embedded derivatives.

CLIENT CLASSIFICATION AND SUITABILITY

APAC clients are classified into three primary categories: Retail Investors (liquid net worth below USD 1 million), Accredited Investors (liquid net worth USD 1-5 million), and Institutional Investors (assets above USD 5 million). Each classification carries distinct disclosure requirements and product eligibility criteria.

For retail investors, the SFC Code of Conduct requires written confirmation of risk tolerance and investment objectives before executing any cross-border transactions. Associates must document client understanding of currency risks, particularly for USD-denominated investments by SGD or HKD-based clients, where exchange rate fluctuations can impact returns by 8-15% annually.

CROSS-BORDER TRANSACTION REQUIREMENTS

All cross-border advisory services must comply with local custody requirements and tax reporting obligations. Singapore-based clients investing in Hong Kong securities must acknowledge potential withholding tax implications of up to 10% on dividend distributions. Conversely, Hong Kong clients accessing Singapore REITs face different tax treatment under the Double Taxation Agreement between jurisdictions.

Associates must ensure clients receive pre-trade confirmations for all cross-border transactions exceeding USD 100,000, including estimated settlement timeframes (typically T+2 for Hong Kong equities, T+3 for Singapore securities) and applicable foreign exchange spreads, which typically range from 0.25% to 0.75% depending on currency pairs and transaction size.

COMPLIANCE MONITORING

Our compliance framework requires monthly reporting of all cross-border advisory activities to respective regulatory authorities. Associates must maintain detailed records of client interactions, investment rationales, and ongoing suitability reviews for all APAC clients. The MAS requires annual suitability assessments for clients with cross-border portfolios exceeding SGD 1 million, while the SFC mandates quarterly reviews for Hong Kong residents with overseas investment exposure above 30% of total portfolio value.

Fees for cross-border advisory services range from 0.75% to 1.25% annually, depending on portfolio complexity and jurisdiction-specific compliance requirements. Additional custody fees of 0.15% to 0.35% annually may apply for securities held across multiple APAC jurisdictions.

This disclosure must be provided to all prospective APAC clients and updated annually or following material regulatory changes. Associates requiring clarification on specific cross-border scenarios should consult the Regional Compliance team before proceeding with client recommendations.
